title: Pepinia patentiflora
intro: Pepinia patentiflora, also known as the Passion Flower or Passion Vine, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its intricate flowers and edible parts. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Pepinia patentiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, such as flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, and alkaloids like harmane and passiflorine. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
